_meta.note: Educational, synthetic-but-plausible case catalog for a training simulator. NOT a clinical reference. IDs are UMLS-style CUIs for realism only. Every case carries a real guideline citation for traceability.

diseases.name: Community-Acquired Pneumonia
diseases.chief_complaint: Cough, fever and shortness of breath for 3 days

history.onset: It started about three days ago and it's been getting worse.
history.cough: Yes, a bad cough bringing up thick greenish-yellow phlegm.
history.fever: I've been running a fever and getting the shakes and chills.
history.chest pain: It hurts on the right side when I take a deep breath or cough.
history.breathing: I get winded just walking to the bathroom.
history.smoking: I've smoked about a pack a day for thirty years.
history.sick contacts: My grandson had a cold last week.

exam_findings.General inspection: Ill-appearing, breathing fast, using accessory muscles.
exam_findings.Auscultate lungs: Crackles and bronchial breath sounds over the right lower lung field, with dullness to percussion.
exam_findings.Examine throat: Mildly dry mucous membranes, no exudate.

Chest X-ray.finding: Right lower lobe consolidation with air bronchograms; no effusion.

Sputum Culture.finding: Gram-positive diplococci; culture pending (likely S. pneumoniae).

diseases.teaching: CURB-65 guides admission. Empiric therapy per IDSA/ATS: amoxicillin or a macrolide for outpatients; add coverage for comorbidities.

diseases.name: Acute Myocardial Infarction
diseases.chief_complaint: Crushing chest pressure radiating to the left arm for 45 minutes

symptoms.name: pain radiating to left arm and jaw

history.onset: The pain hit suddenly about 45 minutes ago while I was mowing the lawn.
history.chest pain: It's a heavy crushing pressure right in the center of my chest.
history.radiation: It shoots down my left arm and up into my jaw.
history.breathing: I'm a bit short of breath and sweating buckets.
history.smoking: Yes, I smoke and my father died of a heart attack at 60.
history.medications: I take a pill for blood pressure but I forget the name.
history.exertion: It came on with exertion and hasn't let up with rest.

exam_findings.Auscultate heart: Tachycardic; a soft S4 gallop is present, no murmur.

ECG.finding: ST-segment elevation in leads II, III, aVF consistent with an inferior STEMI.

Chest X-ray.finding: No acute cardiopulmonary process; no widened mediastinum.

diseases.teaching: Time is muscle. MONA-B plus dual antiplatelet and anticoagulation; door-to-balloon <90 min for STEMI. Avoid NSAIDs and PDE5 inhibitors.

diseases.name: Acute Appendicitis
diseases.chief_complaint: Belly pain that moved to the lower right side, with nausea

symptoms.name: periumbilical pain migrating to right lower quadrant

history.onset: It started around my belly button yesterday and moved to the lower right.
history.abdominal pain: It's a constant ache in the right lower belly, worse if I cough or move.
history.appetite: I have no appetite at all, and I threw up once.
history.bowel: No diarrhea; last bowel movement was normal.
history.menstrual: My last period was two weeks ago and normal.

exam_findings.General inspection: Uncomfortable, lying still, reluctant to move.
exam_findings.Palpate abdomen: Tenderness and guarding at McBurney's point with rebound; positive Rovsing and psoas signs.

CT Abdomen/Pelvis.finding: Dilated appendix (11 mm) with wall thickening and periappendiceal fat stranding; no free air.

Urinalysis.finding: Trace leukocytes, no nitrites, no blood.

diseases.teaching: Diagnosis is largely clinical (Alvarado score). Give pre-op antibiotics and obtain a beta-hCG in anyone of child-bearing potential before imaging. Appendectomy is definitive.

guideline.name: WSES Jerusalem Guidelines for Acute Appendicitis 2020

diseases.name: Diabetic Ketoacidosis
diseases.chief_complaint: Excessive thirst, urination and abdominal pain with heavy breathing

history.onset: I've felt awful for two days with a stomach bug and stopped my insulin.
history.thirst: I can't stop drinking water and I'm peeing constantly.
history.abdominal pain: My whole belly aches and I keep vomiting.
history.breathing: I'm breathing hard and deep even though I'm just lying here.
history.medications: I have type 1 diabetes and ran out of insulin.

exam_findings.General inspection: Lethargic, breathing deeply and rapidly; breath smells fruity.
exam_findings.Examine throat: Dry mucous membranes, poor skin turgor.

Urinalysis.finding: Large ketones and glucose; no nitrites.

diseases.teaching: Fluids first, then insulin, and replace potassium before/with insulin if K+ is low-normal. Never start insulin if K+ < 3.3. Metformin and sulfonylureas have no role in acute DKA.

guideline.name: ADA Standards of Care 2023 — Hyperglycemic Crises

diseases.name: Asthma Exacerbation
diseases.chief_complaint: Wheezing and difficulty breathing that woke the child at night

history.onset: It got bad overnight after playing with the cat.
history.breathing: He's wheezing and can only speak a few words at a time.
history.cough: A dry cough, worse at night.
history.triggers: Cats and cold air set him off; he has eczema too.
history.medications: He uses an albuterol inhaler but we ran out.
history.family history: His mother has asthma and hay fever.

exam_findings.General inspection: Sitting forward, tachypneic, mild intercostal retractions.
exam_findings.Auscultate lungs: Diffuse expiratory wheeze with prolonged expiratory phase; air entry reduced at both bases.

Chest X-ray.finding: Hyperinflation, no focal consolidation or pneumothorax.

diseases.teaching: Early inhaled short-acting beta-agonist plus systemic corticosteroids is the cornerstone. Non-selective beta-blockers can trigger fatal bronchospasm and are contraindicated.

diseases.name: Cellulitis
diseases.chief_complaint: A red, hot, swollen and painful lower leg for 2 days

history.onset: The redness started two days ago and is spreading up my shin.
history.skin: It's red, hot, tight and tender to touch.
history.injury: I scraped my leg gardening last week.
history.fever: I've had chills and felt feverish.
history.medications: I take a water pill for blood pressure.

exam_findings.General inspection: Comfortable at rest, favouring the left leg.
exam_findings.Inspect skin: Warm, erythematous, poorly demarcated plaque over the left shin with mild swelling; a small healing abrasion is present. No fluctuance or crepitus.
exam_findings.Check extremities/edema: Mild pitting edema of the left ankle; pulses intact.

Wound Culture.finding: Pending; Gram stain shows gram-positive cocci in clusters.

diseases.teaching: Non-purulent cellulitis is usually streptococcal — cover with cephalexin. Add MRSA coverage (e.g., TMP-SMX or clindamycin) if purulent. Mark the border to track spread.

guideline.name: IDSA Skin & Soft Tissue Infection Guideline 2014

diseases.name: Acute Pyelonephritis
diseases.chief_complaint: Burning urination, fever and right flank pain

history.onset: I had burning urination for a few days and now a high fever and back pain.
history.urination: It burns to pee and I go constantly; the urine looks cloudy.
history.flank pain: There's a deep ache over my right lower back.
history.fever: I've had shaking chills and a high fever.
history.menstrual: My last period was normal; I'm not pregnant.

exam_findings.Palpate abdomen: Mild suprapubic tenderness; marked right costovertebral angle tenderness.

Urinalysis.finding: Positive leukocyte esterase and nitrites; many WBCs and bacteria; WBC casts present.

Urine Culture.finding: Pending; >100,000 CFU/mL gram-negative rods (likely E. coli).

diseases.teaching: Pyelonephritis needs a systemically-active agent (ceftriaxone or a fluoroquinolone). Nitrofurantoin does not achieve renal tissue levels and is inappropriate for upper-tract infection.

diseases.name: Ectopic Pregnancy
diseases.chief_complaint: Lower abdominal pain and vaginal spotting with a late period

history.onset: The pain started today on my left side and I've been spotting.
history.menstrual: My period is about six weeks late.
history.pregnancy: I might be pregnant; I haven't tested.
history.abdominal pain: A sharp cramp low on the left, and my shoulder aches too.
history.sexual history: I had a chlamydia infection a couple of years ago.
history.dizziness: I felt faint when I stood up this morning.

exam_findings.Palpate abdomen: Left lower quadrant tenderness with mild guarding; no rebound.
exam_findings.Pelvic exam: Left adnexal tenderness and a fullness; closed cervical os with scant dark blood; cervical motion tenderness.

Transvaginal Ultrasound.finding: Empty uterus with a 3 cm left adnexal mass and moderate free fluid in the pelvis; no intrauterine gestational sac.

Blood Type & Screen.finding: Blood type O negative; antibody screen negative.

diseases.teaching: Any person of child-bearing age with abdominal pain gets a beta-hCG. A positive hCG with an empty uterus on TVUS is ectopic until proven otherwise. Give anti-D if Rh-negative. Methotrexate for stable, unruptured cases; surgery if unstable or ruptured.

guideline.name: ACOG Practice Bulletin 193: Tubal Ectopic Pregnancy 2018

diseases.name: Preeclampsia
diseases.chief_complaint: Severe headache and swelling at 34 weeks of pregnancy

history.onset: The headache started this morning and won't go with paracetamol.
history.pregnancy: I'm 34 weeks with my first baby.
history.swelling: My hands, feet and face have puffed up over a few days.
history.abdominal pain: I have an ache under my right ribs.
history.prenatal care: My blood pressure was creeping up at my last visit.

exam_findings.Check extremities/edema: 3+ pitting edema to the knees; brisk reflexes with 2 beats of clonus.
exam_findings.Palpate abdomen: Gravid uterus consistent with dates; mild RUQ tenderness.

Urinalysis.finding: 3+ proteinuria; no nitrites or leukocyte esterase.

diseases.teaching: Preeclampsia = new hypertension + proteinuria (or end-organ dysfunction) after 20 weeks. Control BP with labetalol/hydralazine/nifedipine and give magnesium sulfate for seizure prophylaxis. ACE inhibitors and ARBs are teratogenic and contraindicated in pregnancy.

diseases.name: Acute Cholecystitis
diseases.chief_complaint: Severe right upper belly pain after a fatty meal, with fever

symptoms.name: pain radiating to right shoulder blade

history.onset: The pain came on a few hours after a greasy dinner and hasn't eased.
history.abdominal pain: A steady severe pain under my right ribs that goes to my shoulder blade.
history.appetite: I feel sick and have vomited twice.
history.fever: I think I have a temperature.
history.prior episodes: I've had milder attacks after fatty food before.
history.jaundice: No yellowing of my eyes or dark urine.

exam_findings.Palpate abdomen: Right upper quadrant tenderness with guarding; inspiratory arrest on deep palpation (positive Murphy's sign).

Abdominal Ultrasound.finding: Gallstones with a thickened gallbladder wall (5 mm), pericholecystic fluid and a sonographic Murphy's sign; common bile duct not dilated.

diseases.teaching: Right upper quadrant pain, fever and a positive Murphy's sign with gallstones on ultrasound is acute cholecystitis. Start antibiotics and analgesia; early laparoscopic cholecystectomy (within 72h) is preferred.

diseases.name: Bronchiolitis
diseases.chief_complaint: A 6-month-old with cough, congestion and fast breathing

history.onset: It started as a runny nose three days ago and now she's breathing fast.
history.breathing: Her chest sucks in and she's wheezing.
history.feeding: She's taking less than half her usual bottles.
history.sick contacts: Her older brother has a cold.

exam_findings.General inspection: Alert infant with nasal flaring, mild subcostal retractions and audible wheeze.
exam_findings.Auscultate lungs: Diffuse fine crackles and expiratory wheeze bilaterally.
exam_findings.Check extremities/edema: Capillary refill 2 seconds, mucous membranes slightly dry.

Chest X-ray.finding: Hyperinflation with peribronchial cuffing; patchy atelectasis, no focal consolidation.

diseases.teaching: Bronchiolitis is a clinical diagnosis and management is supportive: suctioning, hydration and oxygen. Bronchodilators, steroids, antibiotics and routine chest X-rays are not recommended and add cost and harm.

diseases.name: Atopic Dermatitis
diseases.chief_complaint: An itchy, dry, red rash in the elbow and knee creases

history.onset: The rash flares every winter and after using scented soap.
history.skin: It's dry, red and unbearably itchy, especially in the elbow creases.
history.itch: The itching is worse at night and disturbs sleep.
history.triggers: Wool, heat and fragranced products make it worse.
history.family history: I have hay fever and my father had asthma.

exam_findings.Inspect skin: Ill-defined erythematous, scaly, excoriated plaques in the antecubital and popliteal fossae with lichenification; skin is diffusely dry. No honey-coloured crust or pustules.

Skin Swab.finding: No significant bacterial growth; no evidence of secondary infection.

diseases.teaching: Atopic dermatitis is a clinical diagnosis. The foundation of treatment is liberal emollients plus topical corticosteroids for flares; topical calcineurin inhibitors spare steroid-sensitive sites. Reserve antibiotics for clear secondary infection.
